solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
1533F - Binary String Partition
We will solve the problem for each value of $$$k$$$ separately.
Note that there is an optimal partition where all $$$t_i$$$ (except the last one) contain exactly $$$k$$$ zeros or ones. Using this fact, we can find in which position the next block $$$t_i$$$ will end. The current block $$$t_i$$$ should end before it contains at least $$$k+1$$$ zeros and at least $$$k+1$$$ ones. To quickly find this position, you can use a binary search with prefix sums, or save all the positions of zeros (ones) in a separate array. Such a solution will work for $$$O(m)$$$ or $$$O(m \log{n})$$$ (depending on whether binary search is used), where $$$m$$$ is the number of strings in the partition.
For each $$$k$$$, the value of $$$m$$$ does not exceed $$$\frac{n}{2k+1}$$$ (because there are at least $$$2k+1$$$ elements in each block). This means that the sum of $$$m$$$ for all $$$k$$$ does not exceed $$$\sum\limits_{k=1}^{n} \frac{n}{k}$$$, which is estimated as $$$O(n \log{n})$$$, which means that the entire solution works for $$$O(n \log{n})$$$ or $$$O(n \log^2{n})$$$.

solutions.title: Solution (Neon)
solutions.content:
fun main
()
{
val s
=
readLine
()!!.
map
{
(
it
-
'0'
).
toInt
()
}.
toIntArray
()
val pos
=
Array
(
2
)
{
c
->
s
.
indices
.
filter
{
s
[
it
]
==
c
}.
toIntArray
()
}
val sum
=
Array
(
2
)
{
c
->
s
.
runningFold
(
0
)
{
cur
,
x
->
cur
+
if
(
x
==
c
)
1
else
0
}.
toIntArray
()
}
println
((
1.
.
s
.
size
).
map
{
k
->
generateSequence
(
0
)
{
i
->
IntArray
(
2
)
{
c
->
pos
[
c
].
getOrElse
(
sum
[
c
][
i
]
+
k
)
{
s
.
size
}
}.
maxOrNull
()!!
}.
indexOf
(
s
.
size
)
}.
joinToString
(
" "
))
}
